name: editor/highlight
description: Read-only syntax-highlighted code block — the same owned tokenizer as CodeEditor, without the textarea.

meta.name: Highlight
meta.description: Read-only syntax-highlighted code block — the same owned tokenizer as CodeEditor, without the textarea.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the code block

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Read-only snippet
examples.code:
import { Highlight } from '@cascivo/editor'
import '@cascivo/editor/styles.css'

<Highlight language="json" value={'{ "ok": true }'} />

whenToUse: Displaying non-editable code or config with syntax colors — docs, snippets, examples
A lightweight highlighter that themes via the cascivo token system
whenNotToUse: The code must be editable — use CodeEditor
You need copy-to-clipboard chrome around a snippet — pair with a code-snippet surface

related.name: CodeEditor
related.reason: The editable surface sharing the same tokenizer

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a read-only <pre><code>; the line-number gutter is aria-hidden and an optional label names the block.
